goal: Ouvrage dashboard design system and UI spec

messages.author: user
messages.title: Design tokens: Copper Forge amber palette
messages.content:
# Ouvrage Design System

This conversation tracks the UI spec for the Ouvrage dashboard.

## Color Palette

Copper Forge uses warm amber on dark brown backgrounds:

- `bg: #0f0d0b` — deepest background
- `surface: #1e1a14` — card surface
- `accent: #d97706` — amber highlight
- `text: #ede8e3` — warm white

## Typography

DM Sans for body, JetBrains Mono for code:

```css
font-family: 'DM Sans', -apple-system, sans-serif;
font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace;
```

## Component Patterns

### Badges

Status badges use colored pill shapes with transparent backgrounds.

### Cards

Cards use `surface` background with `border` outline and `borderRadius.md` corners.

messages.author: cc-worker
messages.content: Implemented initial token definitions in `dashboard/tokens.js`. All colors, typography, spacing, and layout constants are exported.

messages.author: user
messages.title: Tab bar spec
messages.content: The tab bar should have four tabs: Tasks, Conversations, Files, Settings. Active tab gets amber underline. Inactive tabs are textTertiary.

messages.author: cc-worker
messages.title: Tab bar implementation complete
messages.content:
## Tab Bar Done

Implemented `TabBar` component with:

- Amber underline on active tab (`3px solid #d97706`)
- Hash routing integration — each tab maps to `#/project/:id/:tab`
- Badge counts on Tasks and Conversations tabs
- Responsive: on mobile tabs scroll horizontally

All gate tests pass.

messages.author: user
messages.content: Should the conversation index group by pinned status or by project? I'm thinking pinned-first within a project.

messages.author: cc-worker
messages.content: Pinned-first within project makes sense. The `has_pinned` flag from the DB aggregates drives the grouping. Conversations with any pinned message go in the top section with an amber left border.

messages.author: cc-worker
messages.title: Conversation index implementation plan
messages.content:
## Plan

Implement `ConversationIndex` view:

1. `api.getConversations({ project })` on mount
2. Group into pinned / unpinned sections
3. Debounced search via `api.search({ q, project_id })`
4. Cards with amber border for pinned-containing conversations

**Files to change:**
- `dashboard/views/ConversationIndex.js` (new)
- `dashboard/views/ProjectView.js` (wire up tab)
- `ouvrage/db/_helpers.py` (add `pinned_title`)

messages.author: user
messages.title: Review: conversation index
messages.content:
Looks good overall. Two issues:

1. `message_count` was wrong when a conversation had multiple pinned messages — the CTE join was creating a cross-product
2. The `goalStyle` object was defined but never used

Please fix both before merging.

messages.author: cc-worker
messages.title: Handoff: conversation index → detail
messages.content:
## Handoff Notes

Conversation index is merged. For the detail view next:

- Router already handles `#/project/:id/conversation/:convId` — just needs `routes.projectConversation` added
- `ConversationIndex` cards should link to project-scoped URLs once detail view exists
- Reference implementation: `ConversationView.js` handles `#/conversation/:id`

messages.author: user
messages.title: Tab bar spec finalized
messages.content: All specs for the dashboard redesign are locked. Next: conversation detail redesign with TOC sidebar.
